{"found": false, "tool": "verify", "original_ir": "; Test 1: Missing propagation of no-signed-zeros from select
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_no_signed_zeros(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd nsz float %x, %y
  %res = select i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; The nsz flag from the select should be considered when analyzing the pattern,
; but the patch only looks at the fcmp's FMF.

; Test 2: Conflicting FMF between select and fcmp
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_conflicting_fmf(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp nnan olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd float %x, %y  ; No nnan here
  %res = select nnan i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; The select has nnan but the fadd doesn't. The patch only looks at the fcmp's
; nnan flag, missing the select's nnan flag.

; Test 3: Multiple FMF flags from select
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_multiple_fmf(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd fast float %x, %y
  %res = select fast i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; The select has 'fast' which includes all FMF, but the patch only propagates
; no-nans from the fcmp (which doesn't exist in this case).

; Test 4: Original issue - fcmp without nnan but select with nnan
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_original_issue(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd nnan float %x, %y
  %res = select nnan i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; This should be optimized based on nnan semantics, but the patch only looks
; at the fcmp's FMF (which has no nnan).

; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

}

", "thoughts": "Verifying with opt args: -passes=instcombine"}
report:
Looking at this patch, I can identify several issues:

## Issues with the Patch:

1. **Inconsistent FMF handling**: The patch adds an FMF parameter to `matchDecomposedSelectPattern` but only preserves the `no-nans` flag from the fcmp when it exists. This loses other important FMF flags like `no-signed-zeros`, `allow-reciprocal`, etc.

2. **Missing FMF propagation**: When the select has FMF flags but the fcmp doesn't have `no-nans`, the patch doesn't propagate the select's FMF to the analysis, which could lead to missed optimizations.

3. **Incomplete FMF merging**: The code only sets `no-nans` from the fcmp if it exists, but doesn't merge it with the FMF passed from the select. This could lead to incorrect results when the select and fcmp have different FMF.

## Test Cases:

Here are test cases that expose these issues:

```llvm
; Test 1: Missing propagation of no-signed-zeros from select
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_no_signed_zeros(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd nsz float %x, %y
  %res = select i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; The nsz flag from the select should be considered when analyzing the pattern,
; but the patch only looks at the fcmp's FMF.

; Test 2: Conflicting FMF between select and fcmp
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_conflicting_fmf(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp nnan olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd float %x, %y  ; No nnan here
  %res = select nnan i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; The select has nnan but the fadd doesn't. The patch only looks at the fcmp's
; nnan flag, missing the select's nnan flag.

; Test 3: Multiple FMF flags from select
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_multiple_fmf(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd fast float %x, %y
  %res = select fast i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; The select has 'fast' which includes all FMF, but the patch only propagates
; no-nans from the fcmp (which doesn't exist in this case).

; Test 4: Original issue - fcmp without nnan but select with nnan
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_original_issue(float %x, float %y) {
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %x, %y
  %sel = fadd nnan float %x, %y
  %res = select nnan i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %y
  ret float %res
}
; This should be optimized based on nnan semantics, but the patch only looks
; at the fcmp's FMF (which has no nnan).

; Test 5: Integer comparison with FP select
; RUN: opt -passes=instcombine -S -o -

define float @test_int_cmp_fp_select(i32 %x, i32 %y, float %a, float %b) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %sel = fadd nnan float %a, %b
  %res = select nnan i1 %cmp, float %sel, float %b
  ret float %res
}
; The select has nnan but the icmp isn't an FPMathOperator. The patch should
; still consider the select's FMF.
```

The main problem is that the patch doesn't properly merge FMF from both the select and the fcmp. When they conflict, it's unclear which should take precedence. A better approach would be to use the intersection of FMF from both operations (the most conservative set) or document clearly which FMF source takes priority.
